coredata.dc:title: A Novel State of Health Estimation of Lithium-ion Battery Energy Storage System Based on Linear Decreasing Weight-Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm and Incremental Capacity-Differential Voltage Method
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Wu, Zhuoyan
Yin, Likun
Xiong, Ran
Wang, Shunli
Xiao, Wei
Liu, Yi
Jia, Jun
Liu, Yanchao
coredata.dc:description: Accurate estimation of battery state of health (SOH) under energy storage conditions is a key and difficult technology in the use of lithium-ion batteries, which is related to the health and safety of batteries, use efficiency, and product replacement. Because of the complex working conditions of energy storage batteries, it is necessary to use a method to estimate SOH, which takes into account both the internal electrochemical mechanism and the battery degradation mechanism. Based on this requirement, a SOH estimation method for energy storage lithium-ion batteries based on linear decreasing weight-particle swarm optimization (LDW-PSO) algorithm and incremental capacity-differential voltage (IC-DV) method is proposed. The algorithm uses the LDW-PSO method to identify the maximum solid-phase lithium-ion concentration of positive and negative electrodes in the single particle (SP) model, quantifies degradation modes by the IC-DV method, and takes the above parameters as the input of the back propagation neural network (BPNN) to estimate the SOH of energy storage lithium-ion batteries. At 25 ℃, the working condition of an actual energy storage power station is used for simulation and verification. The experimental results show that the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the mean square error (MSE) of the battery SOH in test data are 0.0474%, 0.0261%, and 8.87×10-8, respectively, and the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the MSE of the battery SOH in the same batch with the same degradation path are 0.0077, 0.0012, and 5.24×10-6, respectively. The above results provide a theoretical and experimental basis for the problem that the SOH of lithium-ion batteries cannot be effectively estimated under complex working conditions of energy storage power stations.
coredata.dcterms:subject: electrochemical and degradation mechanisms
state of health
single particle model
linear decreasing weight-particle swarm optimization algorithm
incremental capacity-differential voltage method
back propagation neural network

xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: A Novel State of Health Estimation of Lithium-ion Battery Energy Storage System Based on Linear Decreasing Weight-Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm and Incremental Capacity-Differential Voltage Method Zhuoyan Wu, 1 Likun Yin, 1 Ran Xiong, 2 3 438394119@qq.com Shunli Wang, 3 Wei Xiao, 2 Yi Liu, 2 Jun Jia, 2 Yanchao Liu, 1 1 Science and Technology Research Institute, China Three Gorges Corporation, Beijing, 100038, China; Science and Technology Research Institute China Three Gorges Corporation Beijing 100038 China Science and Technology Research Institute, China Three Gorges Corporation, Beijing, 100038, China; 2 Sichuan Energy Internet Research Institute, Tsinghua University, Chengdu, 610042, China; Sichuan Energy Internet Research Institute Tsinghua University Chengdu 610042 China Sichuan Energy Internet Research Institute, Tsinghua University, Chengdu, 610042, China; 3 School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China. School of Information Engineering Southwest University of Science and Technology Mianyang 621010 China School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China. Accurate estimation of battery state of health (SOH) under energy storage conditions is a key and difficult technology in the use of lithium-ion batteries, which is related to the health and safety of batteries, use efficiency, and product replacement. Because of the complex working conditions of energy storage batteries, it is necessary to use a method to estimate SOH, which takes into account both the internal electrochemical mechanism and the battery degradation mechanism. Based on this requirement, a SOH estimation method for energy storage lithium-ion batteries based on linear decreasing weight-particle swarm optimization (LDW-PSO) algorithm and incremental capacity-differential voltage (IC-DV) method is proposed. The algorithm uses the LDW-PSO method to identify the maximum solid-phase lithium-ion concentration of positive and negative electrodes in the single particle (SP) model, quantifies degradation modes by the IC-DV method, and takes the above parameters as the input of the back propagation neural network (BPNN) to estimate the SOH of energy storage lithium-ion batteries. At 25 â„ƒ, the working condition of an actual energy storage power station is used for simulation and verification. The experimental results show that the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the mean square error (MSE) of the battery SOH in test data are 0.0474%, 0.0261%, and 8.87Ã—10-8 , respectively, and the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the MSE of the battery SOH in the same batch with the same degradation path are 0.0077, 0.0012, and 5.24Ã—10 , respectively, and the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the MSE of the battery SOH in the same batch with the same degradation path are 0.0077, 0.0012, and 5.24Ã—10-6 , respectively. The above results provide a theoretical and experimental basis for the problem that the SOH of lithium-ion batteries cannot be effectively estimated under complex working conditions of energy storage power stations.Keywords electrochemical and degradation mechanisms state of health single particle model linear decreasing weight-particle swarm optimization algorithm incremental capacity-differential voltage method back propagation neural network References 1. simple-head.ce:title: A Novel State of Health Estimation of Lithium-ion Battery Energy Storage System Based on Linear Decreasing Weight-Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm and Incremental Capacity-Differential Voltage Method

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Science and Technology Research Institute, China Three Gorges Corporation, Beijing, 100038, China;
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Science and Technology Research Institute, China Three Gorges Corporation, Beijing, 100038, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Sichuan Energy Internet Research Institute, Tsinghua University, Chengdu, 610042, China;
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Sichuan Energy Internet Research Institute, Tsinghua University, Chengdu, 610042, China;

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Southwest University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Information Engineering, Southwest University of Science and Technology, Mianyang 621010, China.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Accurate estimation of battery state of health (SOH) under energy storage conditions is a key and difficult technology in the use of lithium-ion batteries, which is related to the health and safety of batteries, use efficiency, and product replacement. Because of the complex working conditions of energy storage batteries, it is necessary to use a method to estimate SOH, which takes into account both the internal electrochemical mechanism and the battery degradation mechanism. Based on this requirement, a SOH estimation method for energy storage lithium-ion batteries based on linear decreasing weight-particle swarm optimization (LDW-PSO) algorithm and incremental capacity-differential voltage (IC-DV) method is proposed. The algorithm uses the LDW-PSO method to identify the maximum solid-phase lithium-ion concentration of positive and negative electrodes in the single particle (SP) model, quantifies degradation modes by the IC-DV method, and takes the above parameters as the input of the back propagation neural network (BPNN) to estimate the SOH of energy storage lithium-ion batteries. At 25 ℃, the working condition of an actual energy storage power station is used for simulation and verification. The experimental results show that the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the mean square error (MSE) of the battery SOH in test data are 0.0474%, 0.0261%, and 8.87×10-8, respectively, and the maximum estimation error, the mean estimation error, and the MSE of the battery SOH in the same batch with the same degradation path are 0.0077, 0.0012, and 5.24×10-6, respectively. The above results provide a theoretical and experimental basis for the problem that the SOH of lithium-ion batteries cannot be effectively estimated under complex working conditions of energy storage power stations.

ce:keyword.ce:text: linear decreasing weight-particle swarm optimization algorithm
